candidates.name: Jim Pillen
candidates.notes: 41st Governor of Nebraska (2023-). Catholic. Veterinarian and farmer from Platte County. Signed 12-week abortion ban. Supports school choice. Pro-2A. Opposes sanctuary policies. Self-identifies as Christian conservative.

profile.education: University of Nebraska-Lincoln (BS, Animal Science); Kansas State University (DVM)
profile.background: Veterinarian, farmer, and pork producer; former University of Nebraska football player and regent; became 41st governor in 2023.

candidates.name: Deb Fischer
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Nebraska (2013-). Presbyterian. Cattle rancher. Heritage Action lifetime 70%. NRA A — endorsed and described as 'vocal champion of 2A freedoms.' First woman elected to full Senate term from Nebraska. Pro-life, strong defense hawk.

profile.background: Cattle rancher and former Nebraska State Senator. Runs a ranch in Valentine, Nebraska with her husband. Served in the Nebraska Unicameral from 2005-2013.

candidates.name: Pete Ricketts
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from Nebraska (2023-). Roman Catholic — member Knights of Columbus and Knight of the Holy Sepulchre. Heritage Action lifetime 73%. NRA A. Former 40th Governor (2015-2023). Son of TD Ameritrade founder. Signed Religious Liberty Day proclamation. Draws on Catholic faith for anti-abortion, anti-gay-marriage positions. Pro-death penalty despite Catholic Church opposition.

profile.education: University of Chicago (BA Biology, MBA Marketing/Finance)
profile.background: Former Governor of Nebraska and Ameritrade executive. Son of TD Ameritrade founder Joe Ricketts. Co-owner of Chicago Cubs. Appointed to Senate in 2023 to replace Ben Sasse.
